Cause: Vibration fatigue at solder joint or connector
Effect on System: RPM reads zero; control loop assumes engine stall and drives actuator to low ratio or safe state
Current Controls: Interrupt-driven edge counting detects absence of pulses
Recommended Action: Add strain relief and locking connectors; implement RPM timeout fault flag in firmware

Cause: Loose connector or cracked solder joint from vibration
Effect on System: Sporadic RPM spikes or dropouts causing erratic ratio changes
Recommended Action: Use shielded cable with Deutsch DT connector; add median filter on RPM readings

Cause: EMI from alternator or relay switching coupling into sensor wire
Effect on System: False edge counts inflate RPM reading; actuator drives to wrong ratio
Recommended Action: Route sensor wire away from power lines; add 100nF bypass cap at MCU pin; implement software debounce or minimum pulse width filter

Cause: Adhesive failure under heat and vibration
Effect on System: RPM reads zero permanently; system enters safe state
Recommended Action: Use mechanical retention (clip or pocket) in addition to adhesive; add RPM loss fault code

Failure Mode: Sensor output stuck HIGH or LOW
Cause: Internal Hall IC failure or supply voltage loss
Effect on System: RPM reads zero (stuck LOW) or maximum (stuck HIGH); actuator driven to extreme
Recommended Action: Add RPM plausibility check (compare against expected range for throttle position); add pull-down resistor for defined idle state

Cause: Contamination or mechanical wear of resistive element
Effect on System: Mode selection locked to one value; driver cannot change operating mode
Recommended Action: Add mode override via secondary input or serial command; alert driver via LED indicator

Cause: Vibration or connector failure
Effect on System: ADC reads 0V or floats; mode jumps to undefined value
Recommended Action: Add bounds checking in firmware; default to safe mode on out-of-range reading; use locking connector

Cause: Worn resistive track or dirty wiper
Effect on System: Mode selection flickers rapidly between values causing control instability
Recommended Action: Add hysteresis and averaging filter on ADC reads; require sustained reading before mode change

Cause: Insulation damage from abrasion or pinch
Effect on System: ADC reads rail voltage; mode forced to extreme setting
Recommended Action: Add voltage divider limits check; reject readings within 2% of rail voltages as fault

Cause: Mechanical binding or excessive CVT belt tension
Effect on System: Actuator draws 10-15A stall current; relay contacts overheat; ratio stuck
Recommended Action: Add software stall detection (monitor position feedback for movement within timeout); add per-actuator fuse or current sense

Cause: Vibration fatigue on internal pot leads
Effect on System: Position feedback lost; control loop cannot determine actuator position; may overshoot
Recommended Action: Add feedback plausibility check; halt actuator if feedback is at rail or changes impossibly fast; add redundant limit switches

Cause: Software position limit failure or feedback calibration drift
Effect on System: Actuator internal gear damage; permanent mechanical failure
Recommended Action: Add physical hard stops; add both software and hardware limit detection; recalibrate on startup

Cause: Mud or debris ingress into actuator housing
Effect on System: Ratio locked at current position; stall current drawn continuously
Recommended Action: Add protective boot or bellows over actuator; implement stall current timeout to disable drive

Cause: Thermal damage from repeated stall or manufacturing defect
Effect on System: Actuator unresponsive; ratio stuck at last position
Recommended Action: Implement movement verification after each command; alert driver if actuator non-responsive

Cause: Vibration-induced wiper bounce or EMI on analog line
Effect on System: Position reading jitters causing actuator oscillation (hunting)
Recommended Action: Add RC low-pass filter on A1 (10k/100nF); apply exponential moving average in firmware; add deadband to position control

Cause: Inrush current or stall current exceeding relay rating
Effect on System: Actuator drives in one direction uncontrollably; potential over-extension
Recommended Action: Add relay feedback contact monitoring; implement watchdog that verifies relay state matches command; size relays for stall current with margin

Cause: Inrush current or stall current exceeding relay rating
Effect on System: Actuator drives in one direction uncontrollably; potential over-retraction
Recommended Action: Add relay feedback contact monitoring; implement watchdog that verifies relay state matches command; size relays for stall current with margin

Cause: Software bug or MCU pin glitch during reset
Effect on System: Direct short across 12V supply through actuator motor; massive current draw; fuse blow or fire risk
Current Controls: Software deadtime delay between relay transitions
Recommended Action: Add hardware interlock (series diode logic or NAND gate) preventing simultaneous activation; enforce minimum 50ms deadtime in firmware; verify with scope during testing

Cause: Coil wire fatigue or driver transistor failure
Effect on System: Actuator cannot move in one direction; only single-direction control remains
Recommended Action: Add relay coil diagnostic (read back relay state pin); alert on inability to actuate

Cause: Diode degradation from repeated inductive kicks
Effect on System: Voltage spike on MCU GPIO pin when relay de-energizes; potential MCU damage
Recommended Action: Use TVS diode in addition to standard flyback; verify diode rating exceeds coil energy; add series resistor on MCU GPIO for protection

Cause: Stall current or short circuit in wiring harness
Effect on System: Complete loss of 12V power; actuator and relay logic dead; eCVT inoperable
Recommended Action: Carry spare fuses; add LED fuse indicator; verify wiring harness for chafe points before each event

Cause: Belt slip or alternator failure
Effect on System: Battery drains; 12V rail drops; system brownout and eventual shutdown
Recommended Action: Add battery voltage monitoring on spare ADC pin; alert driver at low voltage; ensure battery can sustain system for remainder of run

Cause: Input overvoltage from load dump or thermal shutdown
Effect on System: 5V rail lost; Teensy and Pi lose power; complete control loss
Recommended Action: Add input TVS diode on buck converter; use automotive-rated converter; add bulk capacitor on 5V output for ride-through

Cause: High current draw from actuator stall coinciding with other loads
Effect on System: Teensy resets or exhibits undefined behavior; actuator state unknown on reboot
Recommended Action: Add 100uF bulk capacitor near Teensy VIN; implement graceful startup sequence with actuator position verification; add voltage monitoring

Cause: Vibration causing wire abrasion against frame tube
Effect on System: Short circuit blows fuse or causes localized fire risk
Recommended Action: Use split loom or braided sleeve on all harness runs; secure with adhesive-lined zip ties at 150mm intervals; inspect before each event

Cause: Vibration and moisture exposure
Effect on System: Intermittent power or signal loss to any subsystem
Recommended Action: Use Deutsch DT or Weather Pack connectors throughout; apply dielectric grease; add secondary locking clips

Cause: Null pointer dereference or stack overflow or unhandled exception
Effect on System: All control outputs freeze at last state or go to reset defaults; actuator may continue driving
Recommended Action: Enable hardware watchdog timer (WDT) with 500ms timeout; implement safe-state on WDT reset (both relays OFF)

Cause: ISR taking too long or blocking code in main loop
Effect on System: System resets unexpectedly; brief loss of control; actuator stops then resumes from unknown state
Recommended Action: Profile ISR execution time; keep ISRs minimal; feed watchdog from main loop only when all health checks pass

Cause: ESD event or voltage spike on I/O pin
Effect on System: Pin output stuck HIGH or LOW regardless of software control; relay may stay energized
Recommended Action: Add series resistors (220 ohm) on relay drive pins; add ESD protection diodes on all external-facing pins

Cause: Electrical overstress or silicon defect
Effect on System: Position feedback and potentiometer reads return garbage; control loop fails
Recommended Action: Add sanity check on ADC values (must be within calibrated range); cross-reference multiple ADC channels for consistency

Cause: Crystal oscillator cracked by vibration
Effect on System: MCU runs at wrong speed or stops; all timing including RPM measurement corrupted
Recommended Action: Use internal RC oscillator as fallback; conformal coat crystal and nearby passives; mount Teensy with vibration isolation

Cause: Integer overflow or divide-by-zero on very low RPM
Effect on System: Computed RPM is nonsensical (negative or extremely large); actuator driven to wrong ratio
Recommended Action: Use unsigned 32-bit math; guard against zero period; clamp RPM to valid range (0-4500 RPM); log fault if out of bounds

Cause: PID gains too aggressive or control loop rate too slow
Effect on System: Actuator oscillates around target; excessive mechanical wear; unpredictable ratio
Recommended Action: Tune PID with step response testing; add derivative gain limiting; implement position deadband of +/- 1mm

Cause: Race condition between extend and retract commands
Effect on System: Both relays briefly energized simultaneously; shoot-through current spike
Recommended Action: Add hardware interlock; enforce state machine with mandatory OFF state between direction changes; unit test relay sequencing

Cause: Unhandled edge case or blocking I/O call
Effect on System: Watchdog fires if enabled; otherwise system hangs with outputs frozen
Recommended Action: Enable watchdog; use non-blocking I/O throughout; add loop timing diagnostic; break complex operations into state machines

Failure Mode: Actuator moves on power-up before calibration
Cause: No startup position check; GPIO defaults to active state
Effect on System: Actuator slams to one end on boot before code initializes
Recommended Action: Set relay pins to INPUT (high-impedance) at very start of setup(); read position before enabling outputs; add startup self-test

Cause: Nested interrupts or long-running ISR blocking higher-priority tasks
Effect on System: RPM count corrupted or timing jitter on control loop; erratic behavior
Recommended Action: Keep Hall sensor ISR to bare minimum (increment counter and timestamp only); disable nested interrupts; use volatile variables correctly

Cause: Vibration or connector fatigue
Effect on System: Data logging lost; no impact on eCVT control function
Recommended Action: Use USB cable with screw-lock or strain relief; log buffer data locally on Teensy SD card as backup

Cause: Pi logging software falls behind or crashes
Effect on System: Logged data has gaps or is corrupted; no impact on control
Recommended Action: Implement flow control or circular buffer; add sequence numbers to serial packets for gap detection

Cause: SD card corruption or kernel panic or overheating
Effect on System: Data logging lost entirely; no impact on eCVT control
Recommended Action: Use reliable SD card (SanDisk Endurance); add Pi health heartbeat monitored by Teensy; enable Pi watchdog

Cause: Feature not developed
Effect on System: No pit telemetry available; pit crew has no real-time data
Recommended Action: Prioritize LoRa implementation; use SX1276 module on SPI; define minimal telemetry packet format

Cause: Distance or terrain or interference at 915MHz
Effect on System: Pit crew loses telemetry; no impact on vehicle operation
Recommended Action: Add local data logging as primary record; use LoRa as supplementary; implement packet acknowledgment and retry

Cause: Driving through mud pits and water crossings (typical Baja SAE)
Effect on System: Corrosion of connectors; short circuits; sensor contamination
Recommended Action: Use IP67-rated enclosure for MCU and relays; conformal coat all PCBs; use sealed connectors throughout; drain holes in enclosure bottom

Cause: Rough terrain and suspension impacts typical of Baja SAE
Effect on System: Connector loosening; solder joint fatigue; component fatigue failure
Recommended Action: Use vibration-dampening mounts for electronics enclosure; lock all connectors; stress-relieve all wiring; use through-hole or robust SMD soldering; vibration test on shaker before competition

Cause: Proximity to engine and CVT during sustained operation
Effect on System: Actuator motor derating; insulation degradation; connector softening
Recommended Action: Add heat shield between engine and electronics; verify actuator temperature rating; route wiring away from exhaust; use high-temp wire (XLPE or silicone)

Cause: Alternator ripple and spark plug discharge coupling into signal wires
Effect on System: Noise on RPM signal and ADC readings; erratic control behavior
Recommended Action: Use shielded cable for all sensor lines; add ferrite cores on power and signal entries to enclosure; use star grounding topology; add bypass caps at MCU pins

Cause: Repeated cold start to hot operation cycles over competition weekend
Effect on System: Solder joint cracking; connector seal degradation; intermittent faults
Recommended Action: Use lead-free solder with adequate thermal cycling rating; conformal coat PCBs; use connectors rated for automotive temperature range (-40C to 125C)

Cause: Dry course conditions with airborne particulate
Effect on System: Potentiometer contamination; cooling blockage on buck converter; connector contamination
Recommended Action: Seal all enclosures; use dust caps on unused connectors; add filtered ventilation if enclosure needs airflow

Failure Mode: Ground loop between Teensy and Pi
Cause: Separate power return paths creating voltage differential
Effect on System: Serial communication errors; ADC offset errors; intermittent resets
Recommended Action: Use single-point star ground; connect Teensy and Pi grounds at one point near power source; use isolated USB if needed

Failure Mode: Total system failure during endurance event
Cause: Cascading failure from any combination of above faults
Effect on System: Vehicle stuck in single ratio or no ratio control; DNF or reduced performance
Recommended Action: Implement comprehensive fault hierarchy; define graceful degradation modes; carry spare electronics module as quick-swap unit; practice field repair procedures

Cause: Linkage pin shears or clevis detaches under load
Effect on System: Actuator moves but CVT ratio does not change; position feedback diverges from expected ratio
Recommended Action: Add ratio validation by comparing actuator position against RPM-to-speed relationship; design linkage with safety factor >3; use cotter pins or locking hardware
